lesson_contract.objective: Prepare and present an offer as a clear next step while protecting accuracy, consent, and handoff quality.
objective_points: Prepare the information needed for an offer
Confirm the next action without pressure
lesson_contract.transition_spoken_text: Take the Closing and Deal Engineering checkpoint next. Then complete the Cadence and Close Capstone.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: you're not the person presenting the offer or negotiating the final terms
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Teach the work without assigning it to or excluding a named role.
Use current-process ownership language with no role title.
Describe the information the next step needs, not a named team.

forbidden_language_removals.exact_source_language: the acquisition team reviews your notes
forbidden_language_removals.replacement_rule: Describe review of the complete record without a named team.
Remove role-bound shirt text from replacement visuals.
Use a generic closing-logistics visual with no role title.

scenes.title: Closing begins before the offer
scenes.spoken_text: A clean close starts with a clean record. Before an offer is prepared, confirm the property facts, the seller's priorities, the people whose consent matters, and the questions that are still open. Missing information creates confusion later.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea standing in a medium shot
visual_plan.editor_note: Use a simple folder as the only prop.

scenes.title: Prepare the offer inputs
scenes.spoken_text: Bring together the condition notes, ownership information, comparable information used by the current process, price expectations, timing needs, and any special request. Mark what is confirmed and what still needs review. Do not turn an assumption into a term.

visual_plan.editor_note: Optional cutaway shows a generic offer-preparation checklist with no job labels.

scenes.title: Protect accuracy and authority
scenes.spoken_text: Know what you are allowed to explain and what needs approval. A draft number, an estimated date, or a possible structure is not a commitment. Use the current written process. If a seller asks for an exception, document it and get it reviewed before you say it is available.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea in a closer medium frame
visual_plan.editor_note: Keep the delivery firm and free of urgency.

scenes.title: Present the written terms cleanly
scenes.spoken_text: When the written offer is ready, explain the terms in plain language. Walk through price, timing, conditions, costs, and responsibilities. Ask the seller to stop you when something is unclear. The goal is understanding, not speed.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea beside a blank offer folder
visual_plan.editor_note: Show no invented amount, date, signature, or provider branding.

scenes.title: Find the unresolved barrier
scenes.spoken_text: If the seller hesitates, do not repeat the pitch. Ask what still feels unresolved. It may be the money, the move, another person, a document, or a condition. Restate the concern and separate what can be answered now from what needs review.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea seated across from an empty chair
visual_plan.editor_note: Use a listening posture and a natural pause.

scenes.title: Confirm consent without pressure
scenes.spoken_text: A valid next step needs informed agreement. Give the seller room to read the document and ask questions. Confirm who needs to sign and whether anyone else should be part of the conversation. Never use urgency that is not real and documented.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea standing at a slight angle
visual_plan.editor_note: No countdown, handshake, or implied signature.

scenes.title: Document the next action
scenes.spoken_text: Whether the answer is yes, no, or not yet, document what happened. Record the open question, the owner of the next action, and when the seller expects to hear back. Send the complete record through the current process so the next person has the same facts.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea at a desk closing a notebook
visual_plan.editor_note: Optional cutaway can show a generic next-action card. No system names.

scenes.title: Keep closing logistics clear
scenes.spoken_text: After written agreement, the work continues through ownership review, required documents, condition or access items, and closing logistics. Responsibilities vary by transaction. Follow the current written process and escalate any mismatch instead of guessing.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea walking past a generic office doorway
visual_plan.editor_note: Remove ACQUISITION shirts and Transaction Coordinator door text. Use no role labels.

scenes.title: Continue to the checkpoint
scenes.spoken_text: Take the Closing and Deal Engineering checkpoint next. Then complete the Cadence and Close Capstone.

visual_plan.shot: Andrea centered in a medium shot

edit_operations.source_problem: Role-bound narration and labels appear throughout the lesson spine. The full recut preserves offer preparation, presentation, consent, and logistics while making ownership reusable and role agnostic.
